titleStmt.title: Letter from Ninian MacIntyre Faulkiner to Thomas Kirkpatrick, 22 October 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Ninian MacIntyre Falkiner

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Dr Ninian McIntyre Falkiner (1858-1929) to Thomas Percy Kirkpatrick (1869-1954) stating Falkiner's resignation from the examiner position within the college. While thanking the college for the honour they have previously bestowed upon him Falkiner writes that he has already given his reasoning for taking this step.Dr Falkiner had served on numerous occasions as an examiner in chemistry for the Royal College of Physicians (RCPI) and Royal College of Surgeons (RCSI). Additionally, during the Great War Falkiner served in England as a temporary captain in the Royal Army Medical Corps at the Lord Derby War Hospital. From 1910 until 1954 Thomas Kirkpatrick served as the registrar for the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland. He also served as the general secretary of the Royal Academy of Medicine in Ireland. He took a particular interest in what were then termed venereal diseases; to encourage his patients (many of whom were prostitutes) to attend, he held a clinic for women at a discreet early morning hour to facilitate anonymity.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.placeName: The Lord Derby War Hospital, Lancashire, England
Royal College of Physicians, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
THE LORD DERBY WAR HOSPITAL Oct 22 nd 1916 Dear Kirkpatrick Thank you for your letter. I would like to
retain the Examinership 'taking the place of an
absent Censor' which is practically honourary. With
regard to the others I wish to ask the College
to accept my resignation, with my grateful thanks
for the honour which has been conferred on me
for so many years, with the confidence of the
College. I have already given you my reason
for taking this step. Believe me
Yours sincerely Ninian M Faulkiner
To T. Percy Kirkpatrick Esq M.D. Fellow & Registrar Royal College of Physicians Kildare St Dublin
